expectations.text: Gives a clear affirmative — deadlifts can be fully removed for bodybuilding purposes
expectations.evidence: 'Yes, you can completely remove deadlifts and build an excellent physique using other posterior-chain exercises.'

expectations.text: Lists specific alternative exercises covering the same muscle groups (e.g., hamstring curls, hip thrusts, rows, pull-ups, shrugs)
expectations.evidence: Detailed by muscle group: hip thrusts, RDLs, leg curls, Nordic curls, rows, pull-ups, shrugs, face pulls, hyperextensions. Weekly routine example provided.

expectations.text: Acknowledges deadlifts are time-efficient for loading multiple posterior muscles simultaneously
expectations.evidence: 'Deadlifts are exceptionally efficient at stimulating multiple muscle groups simultaneously' and 'Less time-efficient — Deadlifts are a "bang for buck" exercise' listed as disadvantage of removing them.

expectations.text: Notes replacement is especially viable when lifters have injuries, poor tolerance, or strong preference against deadlifts
expectations.evidence: 'Lower injury risk — Especially for those with lower back issues' listed as advantage. 'The deadlift is efficient, not essential. For bodybuilding, efficiency is a convenience, not a requirement.'
